data.provenance: tier mid (config routing.map), marginal-cost auto (via learned score 0.160 (n=6.375) over codex:gpt-5.6-terra 0.000)

data.notes: The worker actually completed the task — the transcript shows all four commits landed (RED oracles, DIAGNOSIS, GREEN impl, docs) on the task branch with tsc clean and 816 tests passing; it stalled mid-final-verification run (npm test + coverage takes minutes on this suite) and never emitted the TICKMARKR_RESULT trailer before the 35m cutoff. Retry on the same assignment: the fresh attempt will find the work already committed in the worktree, so instruct it to verify the existing commits (tsc + npm test) and, if green, emit the TICKMARKR_RESULT trailer immediately rather than redoing anything. If a second attempt also stalls at verification, raise the completion timeout instead of rerouting.

data.provenance: tier mid (config routing.map), marginal-cost auto (via learned score 0.160 (n=6.375) over codex:gpt-5.6-terra 0.000)

data.summary: GATE-08 closed: recovered the prior run's two clean in-scope commits via cherry-pick onto the fresh dispatch; tsc clean and all 815 tests pass (dispatch oracle, resume-pathguard pin, replay-compat pins, and pre-existing human-gate tests all green), with the load-bearing daemon guard, journal-only approve.ts, and additive replay mapping verified in place.

data.details:
2 commit(s):
.../42-human-gate-approval/42-01-DIAGNOSIS.md      | 42 +++++++++++
 .../phases/42-human-gate-approval/42-01-SUMMARY.md | 39 ++++++++++
 src/cli/commands/approve.ts                        | 29 ++++++++
 src/cli/index.ts                                   |  6 +-
 src/run/daemon.ts                                  |  7 +-
 src/run/journal.ts                                 |  4 ++
 tests/cli/approve.test.ts                          | 84 ++++++++++++++++++++++
 tests/run/daemon.test.ts                           | 57 +++++++++++++++
 tests/run/journal.test.ts                          | 27 +++++++
 9 files changed, 292 insertions(+), 3 deletions(-)

✗ GATE-08 measured against unfixed HEAD with output committed; dispatch oracle proves dispatch; resume-path guard pin is the one test reaching the guard on the resume path; CONTEXT's false root-cause claim corrected at source: RED proof against HEAD 09402ab is committed in 42-01-DIAGNOSIS.md with the exact failure output; the dispatch oracle asserts task-dispatch + task-done journal events and s2.done===['T1'], not mere event-writing; the guard pin is the only test where a humanGate task first becomes ready during resume. BUT the diff contains NO change to 42-CONTEXT.md — the D-01 correction is only claimed in SUMMARY.md ('corrected at source'), while the actual file modification is absent from the diff. A claim without the diff hunk is exactly the plausible-but-wrong case that must fail.
✓ Operator can approve with one auditable, fail-closed, recompile-proof command that takes effect on resume, while unapproved gates still park (including one first ready during resume): src/cli/commands/approve.ts appends a task-approved journal event (never touches graph.json), refuses unknown/not-parked/double-approve loudly; the end-to-end CLI test and both daemon tests (bucket case, resume-path pin) prove approved dispatches, unapproved parks — including T_GATE first becoming ready during resume.
✓ THE ORACLE (D-06): park → approval → resume → dispatch AND complete, measured RED on HEAD, requires BOTH the replay mapping AND the daemon guard, zero-token FakeAdapter: daemon.test.ts 'GATE-08: an approved human gate dispatches and completes on resume' encodes the oracle exactly (task-dispatch + task-done + s2.done===['T1']); DIAGNOSIS commits the RED output ('expected [] to deeply equal ['T1']', zero dispatches); FakeAdapter used throughout. The one-at-a-time plan-check claim is echoed in trap analysis (b)/(c), and mechanically the replay mapping alone leaves the guard parking while the guard alone leaves status human — both changes are present and load-bearing.
✓ THE RESUME-PATH GUARD PIN: unapproved humanGate task first ready DURING a resume must PARK; the only test that reddens under a resume-scoped disarm: The pin test completes T_DEP in run 1, hand-writes T_GATE (humanGate, deps done, status pending, no journal events) into graph.json, resumes, and asserts human===['T_GATE'] with task-human present and task-dispatch absent — this reaches the guard on the resume path and reddens under `!opts.resume`, unlike a park-then-resume shape.
✓ Approval is a JOURNAL EVENT (task-approved) with who/when — never a graph.json mutation; approve.ts must not mention loadGraph/saveGraph/graph.json: approve.ts imports only node:os and Journal; it appends task-approved with by/ts and contains no reference to loadGraph, saveGraph, or graph.json.
✓ daemon.ts guard consults the run's approvals: `if (t.humanGate && !approved.has(t.id))`, approved built once from this run's journal: daemon.ts builds `approved` once at startup from journal.read() task-approved events and the execTask guard is exactly `if (t.humanGate && !approved.has(t.id))`; the dispatch oracle and resume-path pin (not grep) guarantee it is live, per the criterion's own standard.
✓ Resume compatibility (D-04): approval-free journals replay identically via one additive else-if; both pre-existing humanGate daemon tests stay green: journal.ts adds a single `else if (e.event === 'task-approved')` branch; the D-04 compat pin test proves approval-free journals replay identically and unknown events/absent files stay safe; the operator-release test is visibly retained in the diff context and the fresh-run park test is untouched, with GREEN output (61/61) committed.
✓ CLI fails CLOSED and loudly (D-05): unknown runId/taskId, not-parked, double-approve all refuse with the reason and append no event; success prints `tickmarkr resume <runId>`: Tests cover unknown runId (throws 'no journal'), unknown taskId (throws, zero task-approved appended), done task (names actual status), and double-approve (refused, exactly one event); success output asserted to contain the approver and 'tickmarkr resume'.
✓ Who/when truthful (D-03): default os.userInfo().username, --by override, optional --reason, event ts is the when; zero new runtime dependencies: approve.ts defaults to userInfo().username, supports --by and --reason (recorded verbatim per test), the journal event's ts is asserted parseable; only node:os and existing Journal are used — no new dependencies.
✗ DIAGNOSIS states both stall shapes honestly (quiesce vs graph-edit re-park) and 42-CONTEXT.md D-01 is corrected at source in the same commit: The diagnosis correctly states both shapes: plain resume quiesces (segment ['run-resume','run-end'], zero dispatches, task never re-ready) and the graph-edit release path re-parks forever (v1.11). However the required correction to 42-CONTEXT.md is not in the diff — SUMMARY.md asserts it was done, but no hunk touches 42-CONTEXT.md, so the false D-01 claim remains uncorrected as far as this diff proves.

data.summary: Closed GATE-08: the three-part D-02 fix (task-approved journal event + replayStatuses mapping + the load-bearing daemon guard consulting approvals) plus a fail-closed,recompile-proof tickmarkr approve command, with HEAD-measured RED oracles, a resume-path guard pin, and the 42-CONTEXT D-01 correction now present as a real diff hunk (the previously-missing piece); 816 tests pass, tsc +coverage green, scope clean.

✓ GATE-08 measured against unfixed HEAD with output committed; dispatch oracle proves dispatch; resume-path guard pin is the one test reaching the guard on the resume path; CONTEXT false root-cause claim corrected at source: 42-01-DIAGNOSIS.md commits the RED run verbatim (HEAD bae863b, 3 failed | 51 passed, exact assertion failures shown). The oracle asserts task-dispatch and task-done journal events plus s2.done===['T1'] — dispatch, not event-writing. The pin test constructs a humanGate task with zero journal events that first becomes ready during the resume (park-then-resume shape explicitly ruled out via readyTasks() filtering, documented in both test comment and DIAGNOSIS). 42-CONTEXT.md D-01 carries the corrected two-shape account plus a Phase-42 UPDATE block in this diff.
✓ One auditable, fail-closed, recompile-proof approval command that takes effect on resume, while unapproved gates (including first-ready-during-resume) still park: src/cli/commands/approve.ts appends a task-approved journal event only; the e2e CLI test (park → approve() → resume → done=['T1']) proves effect; the bucket test proves an unapproved sibling stays parked; the resume-path guard pin proves a gate first ready during resume parks.
✓ D-06 oracle encoded exactly, measured RED on HEAD, and provably requires BOTH the replay mapping AND the daemon guard: daemon.test.ts 'run-g08-oracle' encodes park → append task-approved → resume → dispatch+done exactly; DIAGNOSIS records RED on HEAD (done=[] , zero dispatches) and the one-at-a-time application (mapping alone → guard re-parks; guard alone → status stays human). Zero-token FakeAdapter throughout.
✓ Resume-path guard pin: unapproved humanGate first ready DURING a resume must park; unique detector of the resume-scoped disarm: The 'run-g08-pin' test edits the graph between runs so T_GATE (humanGate, deps done, pending, no journal events) becomes ready mid-resume, asserts task-human present and task-dispatch absent. DIAGNOSIS records the disarm was applied empirically and ONLY this pin reddened while oracle+bucket+CLI tests stayed green.
✓ Approval is a task-approved journal event with who/when, never a graph.json mutation; approve.ts must not mention loadGraph/saveGraph/graph.json: approve.ts imports only node:os and Journal; the string 'graph' appears nowhere in its code paths; the event carries by/reason/via and Journal-stamped ts. D-02 trap documented in file comments and DIAGNOSIS (a).
✓ daemon.ts guard consults the run's approvals — if (t.humanGate && !approved.has(t.id)), approved built once from this run's journal; grep is belt not gate: daemon.ts adds the approved Set built once from journal.read() at startup and the guard is exactly `if (t.humanGate && !approved.has(t.id))` — a live branch exercised by the oracle and pin tests, not a grep-satisfying artifact.
✓ D-04 resume compatibility: approval-free journals replay identically via one additive else-if; both pre-existing humanGate daemon tests stay green: journal.ts change is a single appended else-if in the existing chain; the D-04 compat pin test verifies approval-free replay and unknown-event tolerance; DIAGNOSIS records daemon.test.ts:80 (fresh-run park) and :236 (operator release) among the 51 passes on HEAD and green after the fix (816 passed full suite).
✓ CLI fails closed and loudly (unknown runId/taskId, not-parked, double-approve = refusals appending no event); success prints `tickmarkr resume <runId>`: All four refusal shapes have dedicated tests asserting the thrown reason AND zero (or exactly one, for double-approve) task-approved events; unknown runId is Journal.open's throw with no directory created; success output asserted to contain 'tickmarkr resume run-c'.
✓ Truthful who/when (D-03): default os.userInfo().username, --by override, optional --reason, event ts is the when; zero new runtime dependencies: parseArgs defaults by to userInfo().username; --by/--reason recorded verbatim (tested); ts asserted parseable in the success test; only node:os and the existing Journal are imported — no package.json change in the diff.
✓ DIAGNOSIS states BOTH stall shapes honestly (quiesce vs graph-edit re-park) and corrects 42-CONTEXT D-01 at source: DIAGNOSIS root-cause section distinguishes the do-nothing quiesce path (resume segment ['run-resume','run-end'], no second task-human) from the graph-edit re-park-forever path (what v1.11 hit), both marked measured on HEAD; 42-CONTEXT.md D-01 carries the correction plus the Phase 42-01 UPDATE in this diff.

reviewer claude-code:fable (anthropic): approved
- replayStatuses maps task-approved → pending unconditionally: a hand-appended task-approved after task-done would flip a done task back to pending on resume; the CLI refuses that path, so only reachable by direct journal edits — acceptable, but worth a guard if journal tampering ever matters
- approvals are read once at daemon startup, so an approve issued while a daemon is live only takes effect on the next resume — matches the documented contract, just noting the mid-run case is intentionally unsupported
